english: Jang-thang; _broṅ-bri ('brong bri) cow, _broṅ-prug ('brong phrug) calf, _broṅ-ko ('brong ko) skin, leather, _broṅ-śa ('brong sha) flesh, _broṅ-ru ('brong ru) or -ra (-ra) Glr. horns of the wild yak. - 2. v. _braṅ-ba ('brang ba).
noun personal; _brom-stón ('brom ston) a celebrated Lama and scholar in the 11th. century.

pf. and imp. bros (bros), to flee, to run away (W. *šor-ce* (*shor ce*)), _brós-śiṅ gáb-pa ('bros shing gab pa) to flee and hide one's self Dzl.; pýir _brós-so (phyir 'bros so) (the army) took to flight Glr.; _brós-pai ynas ('bros pa'i gnas) Dzl., _brós-sa ('bros sa) Glr. place of refuge; fig. mig ḱuṅ-du bros (mig khung du bros) his eyes are sunk, hollow S.g. - _bros-śa ('bros sha) a large dorsal muscle Med. - yñid-_brós-pa (gnyid 'bros pa) = yñid-lóg-pa (gnyid log pa) (?) Dzl. 724, 9.
v. dba (dba).

1. Sch. a large species of eagles. - 2. W. crutch, = paṅ-ka (pang ka). - 3. = rbab (rbab). - 4. great (?) v. ka-rbad (ka rbad); rbad-sgra (rbad sgra) a strong voice Sch.; cf. rbod-rbód (rbod rbod). - 5. quite, wholly, entirely (?) rbád-gčód-pa (rbad gcod pa), rbad-tsér gčód-pa (rbad tser gcod pa) Mil. to cut off entirely, to extirpate; *tag-čó be'-čó* (*tag co be' co*) resolute C.
Sch. residue, residuum, dregs, husks etc.
1. vb., imp. rbod (rbod), to set on, incite, Tar., C., e.g. ḱyi (khyi); to excite, instigate, animate, Cs.; rbad-ḱa (rbad kha) S.g. an inciting talk (?). - 2. adj. undulating, undulatory Sch.
thick, dense, close, strong, great Cs., skra rbad-rbód (skra rbad rbod) Lex.

1. Med., Sch.: a kind of dropsy, skya-rbáb (skya rbab) Sch., also śa-rbab (sha rbab) Lt. id. (?) - 2. the rolling down, also rbad (rbad), e.g. rdo-rbáb (rdo rbab) loose stones rolling down, a frequent annoyance in high mountains Pth., rbab ži-bas (rbab zhi bas) after the rolling of detritus had ceased Mil.; *bad póg soṅ* (*bad pog song*) a piece of rock rolling down hit him W.; rbab sgril-ba (rbab sgril ba) Lex. to roll down, trs.; rbáb-pa (rbab pa) id. intrs.; már-la rbáb-tu śor (mar la rbab tu shor) it rolled down and away Mil.
Sch. 'the fur of the stone-fox'.
Sch. milt of fish.
v. rbád-pa (rbad pa).

1. wen, goitre. - 2. knots, excrescences on trees, on account of their speckled appearance often worked into drinking-bowls; lbá-tsa (lba tsa) Med., prob. a kind of salt, used as a curative of goitre.
bubbles (?), *ču bag gyáb-cè* (*chu bag gyab ce*) to strike the water, so as to make it splash and foam W.

bubble, foam, froth, slaver; čú-lbu (chu lbu) Lex.; lbú-bčas nyuṅ (lbu bcas nyung) producing little froth Lt.; lbú-ba bsál-ba (lbu ba bsal ba) to scum or skim off Cs.; grogs čú-yi lbú-ba daṅ _dra (grogs chu yi lbu ba dang 'dra) a friend is like water-bubbles.
v. spa (spa).
Sch. a mean house, hovel, hut.
1. vb. fut. of sbed-pa (sbed pa) q.v. - 2. sbst. privy parts, pudenda Stg.
pf. sbags (sbags), imp. sbogs (sbogs) (cf. _bág-pa ('bag pa)), to soil, stain, defile, pollute, drí-mas (dri mas) Lex. - 2. to mingle, intermix, Lex.
v. sbóṅ-ba (sbong ba).

malt from which beer has been brewed, v. čaṅ (chang); sbaṅ-skóm (sbang skom) id. dried, sbaṅ-pýé (sbang phye) id. reduced to flour (of an inferior quality) Cs.; glum-sbáṅ (glum sbang) Ts. = sbáṅ-ma (sbang ma); sbaṅ-ču (sbang chu) barm prepared from it W.

dung of larger animals, rtai sbaṅs (rta'i sbangs) Glr. (*stal-báṅ(s)* (*stal bang(s)*) Ld.), boṅ-sbaṅs (bong sbangs), glaṅ-po-čei sbaṅs (glang po che'i sbangs) Cs.; sbaṅs-lúd (sbangs lud) id., used for manure; sbaṅs-skám (sbangs skam) id. dried for fuel.
C., *sbáb-ja* (*sbab ja*) W. a certain number or quantity of trading-articles, e.g. of paper, a quire of 10-100 sheets, a bundle of matches etc.

pf. sbams (sbams), imp. sboms (sboms), to put or place together, to collect, to gather, pýogs gčíg-tu (phyogs gcig tu) Lex.; smyúg-ma sbáms-pa _dra (smyug ma sbams pa 'dra) like reeds laid together Wdn.
v. sbór-ba (sbor ba).
v. spár-mo (spar mo).
